criteria.criterion: `rebar explain commit-trailer` exits 0 and prints the canonical trailer format (CLI unit test asserts `EXPECTED_FORMAT` in stdout).
`commit-trailer` is listed among known guides in `rebar explain` help and the `unknown-id` error (unit test).

The link-resolution guard passes: across every `AUTHOR_GUIDES` guide body and every `explain_criterion(<id>)` rendering, no markdown link target is consumer-relative; no packaged guide has a bare `docs/…`/`../…` path; every `rebar explain <topic>` cited names a real registered guide/criterion.
`rebar explain review` and `rebar explain plan` no longer reference repo-relative files; sibling guides read as `rebar explain <topic>`, repo docs as `https://github.com/navapbc/…`.
`docs/commit-ticket-trailer.md` names `rebar explain commit-trailer` and `rebar verify-commit-ticket` as CLI discovery paths.
The MCP `explain_criterion` tool docstring names `commit-trailer`, and a guard test asserts every `AUTHOR_GUIDES` key appears in that docstring.
AGENTS.md's trailer bullet names `rebar explain commit-trailer`.

citation.description: All required files are present and properly wired: commit-ticket-trailer.md exists in _guides/, is registered in AUTHOR_GUIDES, referenced in MCP docstring, tests cover drift/link-guard/packaging, and no consumer-relative links remain in packaged guides. No structural issues found. Ticket comment attests local gates green (ruff, mypy, 52 targeted tests).
